EARTH: the planet on which we live
EASE: absence of difficulty; to make less serious
EASY: achieved with little effort; not difficult
ECHO: a sound heard again as reflection
ECOLOGY: study of organisms and their environment
EDIT: to prepare written material for publication
EDUCATE: to teach knowledge and develop abilities
EFFECT: a result or consequence of action
EFFICIENT: achieving maximum productivity with minimum effort
EGALITARIAN: believing in equal rights for all
EGOTIST: person excessively conceited or absorbed in self
ELECT: to choose someone for position by voting
ELITE: a select group superior to others
EMBELLISH: to decorate or add details to
EMBRACE: to hold closely in one's arms
EMERGE: to move out of or away from
EMIGRATE: to leave one's country to settle elsewhere
EMINENCE: fame or superiority that draws attention
EMINENT: famous and respected in particular sphere
EMPOWER: to give power or authority to
EMULATE: to match or surpass by imitation
ENABLE: to give means or ability to
ENCOURAGE: to give support or confidence to
ENDING: an instance of bringing to conclusion
ENIGMA: person or thing that is mysterious
ENORMOUS: very large in size or quantity
ENROLL: to register as member or student
ENSURE: to make certain that something happens
ENTIRE: with no part left out; whole
EPISODE: event that is part of sequence
EQUAL: being same in quantity or value
EQUATE: to consider one thing equal to another
EQUATION: statement that values of expressions are equal
ESTIMATE: to roughly calculate value or number
EVACUATE: to remove from place of danger
EVADE: to escape or avoid by cleverness
EVALUATE: to form idea of amount or value
EVIDENT: clearly revealed to mind or senses
EXAGGERATE: to represent as being larger than reality
EXCAVATE: to remove earth to make hole
EXCEED: to be greater than specified number
EXCEL: to be exceptionally good at activity
EXCERPT: short extract from film or text
EXCESSIVE: more than is necessary or normal
EXCHANGE: act of giving one thing for another
EXCITE: to cause strong feelings of enthusiasm
EXCLUDE: to deny access to; leave out
EXERT: to apply or bring to bear
EXHAUST: to drain of strength or energy
EXIT: way out of building or room
EXPECT: to regard as likely to happen
EXPEL: to force someone to leave place
EXPERIENCE: practical contact with observation of facts
EXPERT: person with comprehensive knowledge of subject
EXPIRE: to come to end of period of validity
EXPIRATION: end of period for which something is valid
EXPOSURE: state of being exposed to contact
EXTENSION: part that is added to make larger
EXTREMES: either of two abstract things as remote as possible
